mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.4% in Dec 2010 suggests ongoing labor market slack
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with declining unemployment trend
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPI) begins to rise, creating negative real rates that incentivize equity allocation over cash holdings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18% in Dec 2010 with CPI at 220.252
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with emerging inflation pressures
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of 60d forward returns, or mechanism fails to outperform single-proxy models by >5% information coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strengthening consumer demand and economic activity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.025 over any 18-month rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 45% of triggered instances despite positive RSAFS growth signal.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, which supports profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 94.8756 in Dec 2010 with medium revision risk
regime_hint: supply-side recovery with improving capacity utilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.015 over any 12-month rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no significant difference between high and low INDPRO growth regimes (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with declining unemployment rate creates a dual labor market signal that amplifies consumer confidence and spending power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both conditions strengthen simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth > 150K jobs AND UNRATE declines > 0.2pp in same period
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130,712K in Dec 2010, UNRATE at 9.4%
regime_hint: synchronized labor market improvement in recovery phase
failure_condition: Joint interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in multivariate model, or mechanism produces lower Rank IC than either single-proxy model over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 500K units signals deteriorating real estate demand and reduced construction activity, which constrains economic growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 555K in Nov 2010, indicating housing weakness persists
failure_condition: Rank IC > -0.02 (i.e., signal direction is reversed or negligible) over any 18-month rolling window, or SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite low housing starts.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rate (below 0.25%) combined with rising industrial production signals monetary accommodation supporting supply-side expansion, which increases equity risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18% and INDPRO at 94.8756 in Dec 2010
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy supporting supply-side recovery
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.12) in logistic model, or combined signal produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows expansion trajectory, which signals sustained economic momentum and rising corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days when growth accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,278.5B (vintage) in Q3 2010 with high revision risk
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion with above-trend growth
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% threshold.
